Kathryn Lloyd (925-548-9114), CSR #5955, kathil58@gmail.com, Court reporter.

The Court announces the tentative ruling. Hearing is held. Argued (as fully noted in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument modifies the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. Plaintiff failed to allege an enforceable contract against the defendant on any facts supporting a duty independent of a contract. Order submitted electronically under review. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: A. Pascual, Kathryn Lloyd (925-548-9114), CSR #5955, kathil58@gmail.com, Court reporter. (D501)

Sushila Chanana; Farella Braun + Martel LLP; 415-954-4400; appearing on behalf of defendant Tenderloin Neighborhood Development Corporation.
Enoch Wang; Fife Law, LLP; 415-837-3101; appearing on behalf of plaintiff Mid-Sunset Neighborhood Association, Inc.

Court reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR $13293, calendar-la@veritext.com, 415-274-9977.

Counsel for plaintiff contests the court's tentative ruling. Mr. Wang ask requests 30 days leave to amend, and the court grants plaintiff's request. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts (not conclusions) supporting each element of each cause of action, including but not limited to, a binding contract, breach of contract and duty.

Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR $13293, calendar-la@veritext.com, 415-274-9977. (D501)

Plaintiff contests the court's tentative ruling. Mr. Wang states that plaintiff will face interim harm if TNDC is allowed to proceed with the current design that has been submitted to the Planning Department, it will be very difficult to reverse that process. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF MID-SUNSET NEIGHBORHOOD ASSOCIATION, INC.'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION is taken under submission. Defendant's counsel to submit a proposed order conforming to the tentative ruling. Plaintiff's counsel to submit a proposed order for consideration.
